issues.body: `_assert_dockerfile_pinned` only tests `[[ "$img" != *@sha256:* ]]`. A Dockerfile such as `ARG BASE` followed by `FROM ${BASE}@sha256:${DIGEST}` therefore passes, even though neither the repository nor this check binds the registry/repository or digest to a concrete value; build arguments can select them later. The same issue permits an arbitrary or empty literal suffix. This makes the added supply-chain check syntactic rather than sound and allows the exact base-image choice to escape review.
issues.fix_hint: Require each external FROM token to be a fully literal image reference ending in `@sha256:` plus exactly 64 hexadecimal characters (while retaining the explicit prior-stage-alias exception), and reject variable expansion in external image references. Add negative self-tests for `${IMAGE}@sha256:${DIGEST}`, a short/non-hex digest, and an empty digest so the bypass flips the suite red.
issues.title: Variable-expanded FROM references bypass the digest-pin assertion

verdict.summary: The directory-prefix expansion closes the identified trust-surface classification gaps, and the added tests are non-vacuous for a simple mutable tag. However, the new Dockerfile assertion is not a sound source-level digest-pin check: it accepts variable-expanded image names or digests merely because the unexpanded token contains `@sha256:`. That leaves a direct bypass in the new trust check, so this needs changes before approval.
